title: Sith acolyte
content:
{{Top}} {{Otheruses|acolyte}} {{TitleOrPosition |image=[[File:Acolyte-MaeWithBackground.png]] |name=Sith acolyte |establishedby= |dateestablished= |datesuspended= |datereestablished= |dateabolished= |government= |organization=[[Sith]]''[[Dark Disciple]]'' |type=[[Dark side adept]] |term= |powers= |duties=*Carry out the orders of a Sith{{Acolyte|Lost / Found}} *[[Dark Acolyte]] {{C|during the [[Clone Wars]]}} }} {{Quote|The Jedi live in a dream, a dream they believe everyone shares. If you attack a Jedi with a weapon, you will fail. Steel or laser are no threat to them. But an Acolyte… An Acolyte kills without a weapon. An Acolyte… kills the dream.|The Stranger, to Mae Aniseya|}} A '''Sith acolyte''', or '''Sith disciple''', was an [[agent]] of the [[Sith]] who existed outside of the [[Sith Master]] and [[Sith apprentice]] relationship of the [[Rule of Two]]. Instead, a Sith acolyte was supposed to be trained as a lesser agent who was not a true Sith. In practice, however, they could be used to overthrow one of the existing Sith. ==Description== ===Overview=== {{Quote|I want a pupil, an Acolyte. But this one… went back on our deal.|The Stranger, on Mae-ho Aniseya|{{Acolyte|Night}}}} [[File:HalcyonLegacy3-AnnieWutextless.png|thumb|left|300px|The vengeful Asajj Ventress was a Sith acolyte with dreams of becoming a true Sith Lord.]] A Sith acolyte, also known as a Sith disciple,''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]'' was a pupil trained in the ways of the [[dark side of the Force]] by a [[Sith]], such as a [[Sith Lord]].{{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}} The Sith{{SWYouTube|4RGDVX3pBgs|The Acolyte | Lightsaber Stunts | Disney+}} known as "[[the Stranger]]" defined a Sith acolyte as an individual who would kill "the dream" of the [[Jedi Order]], the ancient enemies of the Sith,''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Siege of Lothal]]'' without needing to use a physical weapon. Calling on her anger, the Stranger's eventual disciple [[Verosha Aniseya|Verosha "Osha" Aniseya]] would fulfill that requirement by executing [[Jedi Master]] [[Sol]] with a [[Force choke]].{{Acolyte|The Acolyte (episode)}} Under the [[Rule of Two]] designed by the Sith [[Lord]] [[Darth Bane]], the Sith Order was limited to two individuals: the [[Sith Master]] and the [[Sith apprentice]] that master trained.{{TCW|Sacrifice (episode)}} With that in mind, Rule of Two Sith could nonetheless still train individuals in the dark side as their personal agents, with such an agent answering to the title of Sith acolyte instead of [[Dark Lord of the Sith]], Sith apprentice, Sith Master, or even Sith Lord.[[Hyperspace Stories 5|''Hyperspace Stories'' 5]] Instead, a Sith acolyte was—at least in theory—only provided with limited training to serve as a lesser agent who could never arise against the true''[[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition]]'' two Sith: rather than act as one of the Sith Lords, a Sith acolyte acted under their command and carried out what the Sith apprentice [[Dooku|Darth Tyranus]] once disparagingly described as "grunt work." Furthermore, a Sith acolyte could be disposed of once they had outlived their usefulness or had grown too powerful. Due to the deceptive nature of the Sith, however, it was more than possible the Sith apprentice would attempt to train an acolyte to overthrow their master, with plans to take that title for themselves and turn their acolyte into a true Sith apprentice. Tyranus, for example, trained his acolyte [[Asajj Ventress]] as a Sith apprentice{{InsiderCite|219|Zoraida Córdova: The Force of Fantasy}} and told her they would overthrow his own master, the Sith Master [[Darth Sidious]], to rule [[the galaxy]] themselves until Sidious sensed Ventress's growing power and ordered her eliminated, to which Dooku reluctantly complied. ===Duties=== {{Quote|You have done well.''"
"''Your will is mine.|Dooku and Ventress|''[[Dooku: Jedi Lost]]''}} The Stranger's Sith acolyte [[Mae-ho Aniseya|Mae-ho "Mae" Aneiseya]]" also served as a [[Sith assassin]],{{GermanFanCite|114|Star Wars: The Acolyte: There Are Always Four – No More, No Less…|''Star Wars: The Acolyte'': There Are Always Four – No More, No Less…}} as did Ventress.''[[Choose Your Destiny: A Clone Trooper Mission]]'' As a member of Tyranus's [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Ventress also served as a military leader in addition to her assassin role. She held commanding power in the [[Confederacy military]].{{TCW|Ambush}} ==History== ===High Republic Era=== {{Quote|I made a mistake with Mae. I thought she wanted more than just revenge. I thought she wanted what I want.''"
"''What do you want?''"
"''The power of two.|The Stranger and Verosha "Osha" Aniseya|{{Acolyte|Teach / Corrupt}}}} [[File:RevengeJusticeThumbnailAcolyte.png|thumb|right|300px|Mae-ho Aniseya was trained as a Sith acolyte]] During the [[High Republic Era]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]]{{Film|III}} [[Darth Plagueis]] took on{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/the-acolyte-creator-leslye-headland-season-2-heard-nothing-8681155|text=''The Acolyte'' creator says she has heard nothing about getting a potential season 2|author=Ross, Dalton|date=2024-07-26|work=[[Entertainment Weekly Books|''Entertainment Weekly'']]|archivedate=20240725025328}} the [[human]] known only as "the Stranger"{{Databank|qimir|The Stranger (alias "Qimir")}} as his apprentice. In time, the Stranger longed to have an acolyte of his own to train. Further, he hoped to unlock what he called "the power of two"{{Acolyte|Teach / Corrupt}} and for that disciple's aid in overthrowing the [[Jedi]] Order to ensure he could live as he saw fit. At that time, the Sith were thought extinct but had survived in secret{{Film|I}} under the Rule of Two designed by the Sith Lord [[Darth]] Bane, thereby forcing the Stranger to act in the shadows and keep his identity hidden. Eventually, the Stranger found a candidate in the form of the [[witch]] Mae-ho "Mae" Aneiseya, who longed for revenge against [[Brendok four|four Jedi]]—[[Jedi Master]]s [[Indara]], [[Torbin]], [[Kelnacca]], and [[Sol]]{{Acolyte|Revenge / Justice}}—involved in the [[Mission to Brendok|destruction]] of her [[Brendok coven|witch coven]] years prior.{{Acolyte|Choice}} Due to her longing for revenge, she agreed to become his disciple and Sith assassin without question. While she wanted to please him, the Stranger later concluded that Mae only wanted revenge and had no interest in his wider power of two designs. In [[132 BBY]],{{TheAcolyteDate}} acting under her master's orders and hoping to please him, Mae began a [[Mae's quest|quest]]{{SW|url=news/the-acolyte-highlights-lost-found-revenge-justice|text=''The Acolyte'' Explained | Highlights from "Lost / Found" and "Revenge / Justice"}} to slay all four Jedi Masters involved in the Brendok mission, with at least one to be slain without a weapon. However, after she killed Indara and Torbin, Mae decided to abandon the quest after learning her sister, Verosha "Osha" Aniseya, had survived. While she had been trained as a [[Padawan|Jedi Padawan]] under Sol after the Brendok mission, Osha had ultimately abandoned the Jedi path, yet she had agreed to work with the Jedi in the name of stopping her sister. Mae, meanwhile, abandoned her place as the Stranger's pupil with hopes of reuniting with her sister,{{Acolyte|Day (episode)}} yet the Stranger instead revealed himself, slaid Kelnacca himself, and wiped out Sol's Jedi team. Mae switched places with her sister and departed with Sol, while Osha was taken by the Stranger back to his [[The Stranger's island|island base]]. There, the Stranger began to tempt Osha to the dark side of the Force. [[File:MasterAndAcolyte-TheAcolyte.png|thumb|left|300px|Osha joined the Stranger as his new acolyte]] In the end, paths converged on Osha and Mae's [[homeworld]] [[Brendok]], where Osha learned that Sol had killed her mother, [[Aniseya]], during the Brendok incident years prior. While Mae abandoned her longing for revenge to instead see Sol face justice, Osha Force choked Sol in anger and killed him, thereby killing a Jedi without a weapon. The Stranger, meanwhile, learned that Mae and Osha had been artificially created by Aniseya from Sol's conversation with Mae. After the Jedi Master's death, Osha agreed to become the Stranger's new acolyte, whereas Mae allowed her memories of her sister to be wiped, falling into the custody of a newly-arrived Jedi team but unable to tell them anything. All the same, the leader of the newly-arrived team, Jedi Master [[Vernestra Rwoh]], had briefly sensed the presence of the Stranger, who was none other than her former Padawan. While Mae would later be told about her sister's existence by Rwoh, the duo's actions allowed the Stranger and Osha to escape back to his island hideaway. ===Clone Wars=== ====Dreams of Asajj Ventress==== {{Quote|I defeated their plans. Now you shall grant me the title of ''Sith Lord''!''"
"No. ''You destroyed their outpost but you did not defeat ''them''. You must ''prove'' yourself worthy.''"
"''I have ''more'' than done so. Even Grievous couldn't—''"
"I will tell you when you have done so.|Asajj Ventress demands she be granted the title of Sith Lord from her master, Darth Tyranus|}} The [[Invasion of Naboo|invasion]] of the [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]] planet [[Naboo]] by the Sith-aligned [[Trade Federation]] in [[32 BBY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' finally revealed the survival of the Sith Order to the Jedi. First, Sith apprentice [[Maul|Darth Maul]] was dispatched by his master, the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, to the planet [[Tatooine]], where he [[Duel on Tatooine|clashed]] with Jedi Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn]]. Afterward, Sidious dispatched Maul to Naboo, where he killed Jinn but was himself cut down by Jinn's Padawan, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. While Maul actually managed to survive, Sidious moved on by naming [[Count]] [[Dooku]], a former Jedi Master who secretly had already been working with the Sith,{{TOTJ|The Sith Lord}} as his second apprentice, Darth Tyranus. Despite the Rule of Two, Dooku would go onto take an apprentice of his own, the [[Nightsisters|Nightsister]] and former Jedi Padawan [[Asajj Ventress]], who he trained in the ways of the dark side. [[File:VentressPledge1-Nightsisters.png|thumb|right|300px|Asajj Ventress (right) was the Sith acolyte of Darth Tryanus (left), who trained her as a real Sith apprentice in order to overthrow his own master, Darth Sidious.]] So long as Ventress remained a mere agent not trained as a Sith Lord, Sidious was unbothered by her existence and even made use of her in his own plans.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] Formally, she was simply a Sith acolyte and Sith assassin known also as a [[Dark Acolyte]]. However, Ventress's ultimate dream was to be seen as a true Sith with the title of Sith Lord. Indeed, Dooku secretly told her they could overthrow Sidious to rule the galaxy themselves and trained her as a Sith apprentice who would help in overthrowing Sidious.''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Sith]]'' As Dooku's disciple, Ventress served him as a member of the Confederacy of Independent Systems with ranks such as [[Supreme Leader]] and [[commander]]''[[Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy]]'' during the [[Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and Dooku's Confederacy, a war that fed into the Sith Order's [[Grand Plan]]. During a [[mission to Cato Neimoidia]], she also expressed interest in capturing [[Jedi Initiate]] [[Mill Alibeth]], promising her a future under a true master away from the ways of the Jedi. However, she was stopped by [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]],[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] who, along with Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], became one of her greatest foes. Early in the war, Ventress carried out the [[Capture of Outpost 716|capture]] of Republic [[Outpost 716]], a posting even Dooku's greatest [[general]], the [[cyborg]] [[Grievous]],{{TCW|Lair of Grievous}} was [[Attempt to capture Outpost 716|unable to conquer]]. In return for her victory, Ventress demanded the title of Sith Lord, but Dooku maintained that she had not proven herself and instead ordered she [[Hunt for a Jedi artifact|track down]] a [[Unidentified Jedi artifact|Jedi artifact]] they could use in overthrowing Sidious. In the end, however, she gave up on the quest, having deduced that Dooku only cared for his own interests instead of her own. Upon returning to him empty-handed, Dooku declared she would never become a real Sith Lord and left her, to which she privately stated she would never give him what he desired. Despite that proclamation, Ventress would decide to continue loyally serving him as his apprentice, with Dooku continuing her training. ====Betrayal of Asajj Ventress==== {{Quote|There is a disturbance in the Force. Your assassin, she has become very powerful. ''[…]'' I would hate to think you are training your own Sith apprentice to destroy me…''"
"''Never! My allegiance is to you and you alone.''"
"''Then you must prove it. Eliminate her.''"
"''She's my most trusted—''"
"''I said eliminate her!|Darth Sidious and Darth Tyranus, on Asajj Ventress|}} [[File:Nightsisters-VentressandDooku.png|thumb|left|300px|During the Battle of Sullust, Count Dooku left Asajj Ventress for dead and attempted to kill her on the orders of Darth Sidious.]] In [[21 BBY]], Sidious sensed Ventress's growing power and, recognizing that Dooku was using her as a Sith apprentice who would help in overthrowing him, ordered Dooku to eliminate Ventress. While he initially objected, Dooku obeyed and ordered [[Confederacy military|Separatist forces]] under her command at the [[Battle of Sullust]] to turn on the assassin, whom he contacted via [[hologram]] to reveal she was no longer his apprentice. Believing her dead, Dooku contacted Sidious to reveal he had followed through with his master's demands, much to Sidious's delight. Secretly, however, Ventress survived and returned to her homeworld of [[Dathomir]], where she reunited with the Nightsisters and plotted to enact revenge on Dooku. Her ultimate plan involved allowing Dooku to take on the [[Nightbrothers|Nightbrother]] [[Savage Opress]]—who was, unknown to her, the brother of Maul{{TCW|Witches of the Mist}}—as his new apprentice{{TCW|Monster}} and Dark Acolyte, in which capacity he would be trained by and get close to Dooku. Unknown to Dooku, Opress would be a sleeper agent who Ventress would activate when the time was right. As he had with Ventress, Dooku planned to train Opress as a Sith apprentice who would help him in overthrowing Sidious, but his failure to capture [[King]] [[Katuunko]] alive during a [[Showdown at Toydaria|mission]] to the planet [[Toydaria]] enraged Dooku. Dooku only promised Opress redemption when Ventress suddenly revealed herself aboard their [[Dooku's Providence-class carrier/destroyer|''Providence''-class Dreadnought]]. While Ventress activated Opress, he broke free of the Nightsister spell and betrayed both Ventress and Dooku, ultimately leaving on a quest to find his brother Maul, with whom he would forge a splinter Sith Order that Sidious later put down{{TCW|The Lawless}} in [[19 BBY]]. Meanwhile, Ventress remained a fugitive from the Confederacy and was branded a traitor, leading to the [[Battle of Dathomir|eradication of the Nightsisters]] on Dooku's orders{{TCW|Massacre}} in [[20 BBY]]. She then took up the life of a bounty hunter and, in 19 BBY, joined Jedi Master [[Quinlan Vos]] in a [[Mission to Raxus Secundus|mission to assassinate Dooku]]. [[File:Dark_Disciple_Cover_Detail.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Ventress with her lover Quinlan Vos, who would fall to the dark side as Dooku's third apprentice]] In their work together, Ventress and Vos fell in love before finally making their move against Dooku on the CIS [[capital]] world of [[Raxus Secundus]]. The mission ended in failure, however; Dooku captured Vos and tempted him to the dark side as his newest apprentice, with Dooku later heralding Vos as the Dark Lord of the Sith Ventress could never become. While Vos hoped to use Dooku to get close to and wipe out Sidious, his plan brought him into the fold of the dark side, which he only broke from when Ventress seemingly{{TBB|The Harbinger}} sacrificed herself to protect him during the [[second battle of Christophsis]]. Much like Vos, whose fall into darkness Kenobi blamed on himself and the [[Jedi High Council]] at large for approving an assassination mission in the first place, Ventress also found herself returned to the light side through her actions{{Topps|set=2019 Topps Star Wars Masterwork|subset=The Dark Side|cardname=Asajj Ventress}} and survived past Dooku's own demise{{Film|III}} as an independent agent active during the [[Imperial Era]]. ===Legacy=== In his book ''[[The Secrets of the Jedi]]'', Jedi Master [[Luke Skywalker]] reflected on Ventress, referring to her as a Sith disciple. Sidious himself also reflected on Dooku's efforts to usurp him with Ventress, Opress, and finally Vos in his book ''[[The Secrets of the Sith]]''. ==Behind the scenes== ===Development=== [[File:Sith_Adept.jpg|thumb|left|300px|An unidentified Sith Acolyte in ''Star Wars'' Legends]] The position of Sith acolyte first appeared in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] through the character of Asajj Ventress in the [[2008]] film ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', although the title of "Sith acolyte" was not used to describe her until the [[2015]] reference book ''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'', where she was described as a "Sith-acolyte-turned-bounty-hunter."''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' The 2015 novel ''[[Dark Disciple]]'' by [[Christie Golden]] then used the phrase "Sith acolyte" on its own, confirming the hyphen seen in ''Ultimate Star Wars'' was only used for grammatical purposes instead of being part of formal name. At [[Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities]] at ''[[Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge]]'', a [[Unidentified mask|mask]] is featured and said by some attendants to be a Sith acolyte mask, while others call it the [[Mask of Lord Momin|mask]] of the [[Sith Lord]] [[Darth Momin]].{{GEAttr|Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities}} has backstory elements that, though not directly published for the public, are officially [[canon]] details that can be obtained verbally from cast members working in the store. The mask does not resemble that latter item, however.[[Lando 3|''Lando'' 3]] The rank of [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith acolyte]],{{TORcite|type=codex|Sith Titles}} whose holders were also identified as Sith adepts,''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' also existed in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity as a position in the pre-[[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], where they held a disciple position. In Legends, the Rule of Two-era Sith also continued to make use of the term for certain lesser agents,''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'' including the [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Legends incarnation of Asajj Ventress]],[[Star Wars: Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Lords of the Sith]] until the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] restored the [[One Sith|Sith Order as a organization with multiple Sith]], including acolytes.[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]] In Legends, the Sith Acolyte position first appeared through Ventress in the [[2003]] comic ''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'',''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'' but she would not be outright confirmed as a Sith Acolyte in Legends until [[Star Wars: Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars Insider: Lords of the Sith'']], a [[2017]] ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' compilation that included the Legends-era article "[[The Sultry Sith]]" by [[Jonathan Wilkins]], whose work was originally published in [[Star Wars Insider 122|''Star Wars Insider'' 122]] from [[December 14]], [[2010]].{{SWArchive|url=fans/media_news/star_wars_insider_122/index.html|text=Exclusive Howard Chaykin Cover and Poster in ''Star Wars Insider'' #122}} In its contents page, the 2017 compilation referred to Ventress as a Sith Acolyte in its blurb for "The Sultry Sith." ===Non-canon history=== {{Quote|We are acolytes of the Sith and sworn to the dark side. And we are here to put an end to your pitiful plans of restoring the Jedi Order!|Hanbei, to Margrave Juro|audio=HanbeiAcolytes-NinthJedi.mp3|{{Visions|The Ninth Jedi}}}} A grouping of Sith identified as "acolytes of the Sith" by the acolyte [[Hanbei]] appear in the non-canon short film "[[The Ninth Jedi]]," which premiered on [[September 22]], [[2021]],{{SWYouTube|b9Gr9gKFxpE|STAR WARS: VISIONS | SPECIAL LOOK | DISNEY+|sw_url=video/special-look-star-wars-visions}} and depicts a vision of the galaxy in which the Jedi Order has been all but destroyed while a new era of Sith rule is plotted by the acolytes. The acolytes made their way to the [[Margrave]] [[Juro]] on behalf of the Sith Order so as to build lightsabers for their cause, only for their number to be wiped out by the re-building Jedi Order.
